header.title: B2B vs UoP Calculator {{year}}
header.subtitle: Compare your earnings and choose the best option

form.ip_box_qualified_share_tooltip: Percentage of income qualifying as qualified IP rights. The remaining part is taxed using the selected base form.
form.ip_box_warning: IP Box requires records of qualified income and calculating the nexus ratio. Consult your accountant before choosing this form.
form.kup_standard_tooltip: Standard tax-deductible costs for employees (250 PLN/month, 3000 PLN/year).
form.kup_elevated_tooltip: Elevated tax-deductible costs for employees commuting from another town (300 PLN/month, 3600 PLN/year).
form.kup_creative_50_tooltip: 50% tax-deductible costs for creative work, up to an annual limit of 120,000 PLN.
form.uop_to_b2b_mode: UoP to B2B (Calculate B2B invoice to match UoP)
form.b2b_to_uop_mode: B2B to UoP (Calculate UoP salary to match B2B)
form.kup_tooltip: Tax-deductible costs (KUP) are expenses incurred to generate revenue. Standard KUP for employees is 250 PLN/month (3000 PLN/year). For employees commuting from another town, it is 300 PLN/month (3600 PLN/year). Creators can benefit from 50% KUP, but not more than 120,000 PLN per year.

results.title: Calculation Results
results.solidarity_tax_disclaimer: Note: the 4% solidarity tax applies to total income above 1,000,000 PLN from all sources. This calculator assumes one source; include all income in the PIT-DS declaration.
results.break_even_subtitle: (This is the monthly B2B invoice amount needed to match the total annual value of the UoP contract)
results.break_even_b2b_subtitle: (This is the monthly B2B invoice amount needed to match the total annual value of the UoP contract)
results.break_even_uop_subtitle: (This is the monthly UoP gross salary needed to match the total annual value of the B2B contract)
results.lost_revenue_tooltip: Lost revenue is the amount B2B loses when not working (vacation, sick leave, downtime).
results.pension_info_limits: 2026 limits: IKE {{ike}} PLN, IKZE {{ikzeStandard}} PLN (B2B: {{ikzeB2b}} PLN).

sec.timeoff_sub: Days without income counted as loss

field_help.employer_budget: Total amount the company is willing to spend on this position. For B2B = invoice amount (company pays net; they reclaim VAT). For UoP = total employer cost / super-gross (gross + employer ZUS ~20.5%: pension 9.76%, disability 6.50%, accident 1.67%, FP/FS 2.45%, FGŚP 0.10%). Large IT firms ≥10 employees have 0.67% accident rate (≈19.5%). If PPK selected: +1.5%. All other B2B and UoP settings still apply.
field_help.monthly_invoice: Net amount on VAT invoice per month
field_help.voluntary_sick: Sickness contribution (2.45% of ZUS base) grants sick pay eligibility
field_help.ip_share: Share of qualified IP income — remainder taxed at base rate
field_help.youth_relief: PIT-0 tax relief for persons under 26
field_help.annual_bonus: % of annual gross — e.g. 10% = one extra month. Added to net after income tax.
field_help.holidays_paid: Check if your contract has a fixed monthly rate — Polish public holidays (~13 days/year) do not reduce your income. Uncheck if you don't invoice on public holidays.
field_help.paid_vacation: Days paid by your client/employer — do not reduce your revenue. For a fixed monthly rate, enter the same number as total vacation days.
field_help.uop_custom_benefits: Annual value of non-wage benefits (healthcare, sport card, training, etc.) — enter a value to include them manually.
field_help.stoppage_months: Months without clients/projects, no B2B income

tooltip.effective_rate: Income tax divided by gross revenue. Does not include ZUS contributions or business costs.
tooltip.breakeven: The B2B invoice amount (or UoP gross salary) at which both contract forms yield identical total value — at current ZUS, tax, and cost settings.
tooltip.lost_revenue: B2B contractors have no paid leave. Every day off = no invoice. 20 vacation days ≈ 8.3% of annual revenue lost.

res.breakeven_sub: At what B2B rate results are equal
res.footnote: Calculations based on 2026 regulations. Not tax advice.
